commands.review_summary:
This PR scopes Control UI local assistant avatar override storage by agent ID and threads the agent ID through render, assistant-identity, and bootstrap callers with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +64. Total +73 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source. Current main and v2026.6.6 read and write all local assistant avatar overrides through one unscoped `openclaw.control.assistant.v1` key, which matches the linked multi-agent overwrite report.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human follow-up for contributor browser proof and canonical-branch selection among duplicate avatar PRs; automation should not repair around missing external proof.

target.title: fix(ui): scope local assistant avatar override to agent ID (fixes #90890)
target.body:
## Summary

Local assistant avatar overrides were stored under a single global `localStorage` key (`openclaw.control.assistant.v1`), so setting an avatar for one agent changed it for all agents. This PR scopes the override to the current agent ID.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/storage.ts`: `loadLocalAssistantIdentity` / `saveLocalAssistantIdentity` now accept an optional `agentId` parameter. When provided, the storage key becomes `openclaw.control.assistant.v1:<agentId>`.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.ts`: Threads `state.assistantAgentId` through `loadAssistantIdentity` and `setAssistantAvatarOverride`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`: Passes `state.assistantAgentId` when loading / saving the local avatar override.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.ts`: Applies the agent-scoped local override during bootstrap.

## Backward compatibility

When no `agentId` is provided, the functions still fall back to the legacy global key, preserving existing behavior for unscoped callers.

## Verification

- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/assistant-identity.test.ts` — passed (6 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts` — passed (16 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/views/config-quick.test.ts` — passed (14 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts` — passed (20 tests)
- `pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts` — passed (10 tests)

Fixes #90890

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Setting a local assistant avatar override for one agent overwrote the avatar for every agent because the override was stored under a single global `localStorage` key.

**Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout of this branch, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, jsdom (Control UI test environment).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /home/0668000780/开源社区提交2/openclaw
git checkout fix/per-agent-avatar-override
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/repro-90932.proof.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose
```

The proof test calls `saveLocalAssistantIdentity` and `loadLocalAssistantIdentity` for two different agent IDs using a real in-memory `localStorage` implementation and verifies that updating one agent does not affect the other.

**Evidence after fix:**

```
stdout | ui/src/ui/repro-90932.proof.test.ts > real behavior proof #90932 > scopes local assistant avatar override to agent ID
=== Before saving avatars ===
localStorage keys: []

=== After saving avatars ===
localStorage keys: [
  'openclaw.control.assistant.v1:agent-a',
  'openclaw.control.assistant.v1:agent-b'
]
  openclaw.control.assistant.v1:agent-a: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-a-avatar.png\"}
  openclaw.control.assistant.v1:agent-b: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-b-avatar.png\"}

=== Loaded identities ===
agent-a: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-a-avatar.png\"}
agent-b: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-b-avatar.png\"}

=== After updating agent-b only ===
agent-a: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-a-avatar.png\"}
agent-b: {\"avatar\":\"https://example.com/agent-b-new.png\"}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Each agent gets its own `localStorage` key (`openclaw.control.assistant.v1:<agentId>`), and changing the avatar for `agent-b` leaves `agent-a` unchanged.

**What was not tested:** A live browser screenshot of the Control UI avatar settings panel; the key-scoping behavior is exercised directly against the real storage functions.
target.author: xydigit-sj
